comments.body: Superseded by epic 4702-a55c-7aeb-445a (dark-acme-lumen): 'Additive, kind-keyed attestations'. The fix is generalized there (attestations{} kind-keyed map, per-kind verify, immutable-record + validity-on-read, future-kind + CI-readiness) and decomposed into 6 stories. Tracking moves to the epic.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "superseded by epic dark-acme-lumen". Session: d755bfe4e.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
A work ticket carries a single cryptographic attestation slot (`state["signature"]`). Both the **plan-review attestation** (earned at claim time via `rebar review-plan`) and the **completion verdict** (signed at close by the completion verifier) are written as `SIGNATURE` events into that one slot. The reducer applies `SIGNATURE` as last-writer-wins, so the completion verdict signed on close **overwrites and destroys** the plan-review attestation. These two attestations are independent proofs of two different gates and should be **additive** — a closed ticket should retain BOTH.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Take a non-bug work ticket (e.g. a `story`) and earn a plan-review attestation: `rebar review-plan <id>` (PASS). `rebar show <id>` now shows `signature.manifest[0] == "plan-review: PASS"`.
2. Claim and complete the work, then close: `rebar transition <id> in_progress closed`. The completion verifier runs and signs a completion verdict.
3. `rebar show <id>` again.

**Expected:** the ticket retains both attestations — the plan-review PASS *and* the completion-verifier PASS (queryable independently).

**Actual:** the `signature` field now shows only `manifest[0] == "completion-verifier: PASS"`; the plan-review attestation is gone from compiled state. Observed live on story `3d3d-da49-c3f3-4298` (REB-522): `signature` was `["plan-review: PASS", ...]` while in_progress and became `["completion-verifier: PASS", ...]` after close.

## Root Cause
`src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:376` `process_signature()` does `state["signature"] = {…}` — a single slot, last-writer-wins (see its own docstring, line 377). The compactor builds the SNAPSHOT `compiled_state` via this same reducer, so after compaction the earlier attestation is permanently lost from compiled state (the raw append-only SIGNATURE events still exist in the log, but `show`/MCP/`verify-signature`/the gates read the single compiled field). Both verdict kinds are already distinguishable by `manifest[0]` (`"plan-review: …"` vs `"completion-verifier: …"`), so they can be keyed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A ticket can simultaneously hold a plan-review attestation AND a completion verdict; signing one does not clobber the other (additive, not last-writer-wins across kinds).
- [ ] Within a single kind, last-writer-wins still applies (re-running plan-review replaces the prior plan-review attestation; re-closing replaces the prior completion verdict).
- [ ] `process_signature` (and the SNAPSHOT/compaction path that replays it) preserves all kinds across compaction.
- [ ] `verify-signature` can certify each attestation kind; the claim gate reads the plan-review attestation and the close gate reads/writes the completion verdict, each unaffected by the presence of the other.
- [ ] Backward compatible: tickets with a single legacy `signature` still verify; the change is forward/back-compat for older clones (new shape preserved-and-ignored, per the rollout convention).
- [ ] Tests cover: plan-review then close retains both; re-sign same kind replaces in place; round-trip through compaction; legacy single-signature ticket still reads/verifies.

## Affected Files (initial)
- `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` (`process_signature`)
- `src/rebar/signing.py` (`sign_manifest`, verify)
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`, `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` (signing call sites)
- relevant SIGNATURE schema under `src/rebar/schemas/` and reducer/compaction tests
compiled_state.title: Completion verdict signature clobbers the plan-review attestation (single signature slot is last-writer-wins; should be additive)
